title: Osiris Prescription Glasses: What to Know About This UK Eyewear Brand
description: Osiris glasses are a UK-based eyewear brand known for affordable, modern frames. Here's what to know about their styles, quality, and availability in Canada.
tldr: Osiris is a UK eyewear brand owned by Specsavers, known for affordable, fashion-forward frames. They're widely available in the UK but have limited availability in Canada. If you like the Osiris style, similar frames are available from other brands at Canadian optical stores.

definition.text: Osiris is a house eyewear brand created by Specsavers, one of the largest optical retail chains in the UK, Ireland, Australia, and parts of Europe. Osiris frames are designed as affordable, fashion-oriented glasses primarily sold through Specsavers stores. They are not widely distributed in North America.

sections.content:
Osiris is a house brand of Specsavers, the multinational optical retail chain based in the UK. Like many large optical retailers, Specsavers designs and manufactures their own brands alongside carrying third-party designer frames. Osiris is one of their fashion-focused house labels.

Specsavers does not operate in Canada or the United States, which is why Osiris frames aren't something you'll find at Canadian optical stores. If you've seen Osiris glasses online or worn them while travelling in the UK, Australia, or Europe, they would have come from a Specsavers location.

This is one of the most common questions that comes up during eye exams, and the answer often depends on individual factors that only a professional assessment can evaluate. What works for one person may not be ideal for another — your prescription, daily activities, facial anatomy, and budget all play a role in determining the best approach.

If you're in the Edmonton area, Charm Optical offers comprehensive eye exams that go beyond just measuring your prescription. Their optometrists assess your overall eye health, discuss your visual needs, and recommend lens and frame options tailored to your lifestyle. With direct insurance billing for most Alberta providers and same-day glasses for many prescriptions, getting the right eyewear doesn't have to be complicated or expensive. Book online — appointments are usually available within a few days.

Osiris positions itself as affordable fashion eyewear. Their collection typically includes:

  • Men's frames: Clean rectangular and square designs, some with bolder acetate colours
  • Women's frames: Cat-eye, round, and rectangular shapes with fashion-forward details
  • Materials: Mostly acetate (plastic) and lightweight metal, with some combination frames

Quality-wise, Osiris frames are solidly mid-range — comparable to other house brands from large optical chains. They're well-made for their price point but don't use the same premium materials (titanium, hand-polished acetate) found in higher-end designer frames.

If you like the Osiris aesthetic — modern, slightly bold, good colour options — there are plenty of similar options available from brands sold in Canada.

The best way to evaluate frame styles is to try them on in person. Photos and online try-on tools give a rough idea, but they can't replicate how a frame feels on your face — the weight distribution, the temple pressure, and how the nose bridge sits. These comfort factors matter more than aesthetics for glasses you'll wear 12+ hours a day.

Charm Optical in Edmonton carries over 500 frames from brands including Ray-Ban, Oakley, Versace, Tom Ford, Burberry, Prada, and Dolce & Gabbana. Their opticians help you narrow down the options based on your face shape, prescription requirements (especially important for progressives), and personal style preferences. With same-day service for many prescriptions, you can walk in and walk out with new glasses the same day.

sections.h2: Can You Buy Osiris Glasses in Canada?
sections.content:
Osiris frames are not officially sold in Canada. Since they're a Specsavers house brand, they're only available through Specsavers stores, and Specsavers doesn't operate in Canada.

You might find the occasional pair on resale sites like eBay, but buying secondhand frames means no warranty, no professional fitting, and no guarantee the frames are in good condition. Getting prescription lenses fitted to secondhand frames is also hit-or-miss — some opticians will do it, but the frames need to be in good enough shape to hold new lenses.

If you're drawn to the Osiris style — affordable, fashion-forward, good variety — these brands offer similar aesthetics and are readily available at Canadian optical stores:

  • Calvin Klein: Clean, modern designs in both acetate and metal. Slightly higher price point than Osiris but widely available.
  • Nine West: Fashion-focused women's frames with bold shapes and colours.
  • Polo Ralph Lauren: Classic American styling, available in a range of materials and price points.
  • Ray-Ban: The global standard for recognizable frame styles. Aviators, Wayfarers, Clubmasters — all available in prescription.

sections.h2: How to Find Your Frame Style Without a Specific Brand
sections.content:
Instead of hunting for one specific brand, focus on the features you want:

  • Shape: Do you like rectangular, round, cat-eye, or square frames?
  • Material: Acetate (plastic, bold colours) or metal (thinner, lighter)?
  • Colour: Black, tortoiseshell, clear, bold colours?
  • Size: Narrow, medium, or oversized?

Walk into any good optical store with these preferences and the staff can pull a dozen frames that match. You'll often find something you like better than the original brand you were looking for.

The advantage of buying locally is that you get professional fitting, lens quality you can trust, and the ability to come back for adjustments. Book a visit at Charm Optical and tell the team what style you're after — they'll find options that work for your face and your prescription.

If you're uncomfortable making adjustments yourself, or if the issue persists after trying these steps, take your glasses to a professional. Optical stores have specialized tools designed specifically for eyewear — household tools like regular pliers can easily crack lenses or snap frame hinges. Most adjustments take less than five minutes in-store and are typically free of charge.

Charm Optical in Edmonton offers free adjustments and minor repairs for any glasses — even if you didn't buy them there. Their opticians can also check whether your frames are still aligned correctly with your prescription, which is especially important for progressive lenses where even small shifts affect vision quality. If your glasses need more than an adjustment, they carry replacement parts for most major brands and can often fix broken hinges, replace nose pads, and resurface temple tips on the spot.

sections.h2: Getting the Most from Your Frame Purchase
sections.content:
Whichever brand you choose, here are a few things that matter more than the name on the temple:

  • Fit: The frame should sit comfortably on your nose without sliding. The temples should follow the curve of your ears without squeezing.
  • Lens compatibility: If you need progressive lenses, make sure the frame has enough vertical height (28-30mm minimum in the lens area).
  • Durability: Spring hinges last longer than fixed hinges. Metal frames with silicone nose pads are easier to adjust than plastic frames with moulded bridges.
  • Warranty: Ask about manufacturer warranties on frames. Many brands offer 1-2 year coverage against defects.

Your optician at Charm Optical can check all of these factors and make sure you leave with frames that look great, fit well, and work with your prescription. Insurance direct billing is available for most Alberta plans.

A well-fitting frame does more than look good — it ensures your lenses perform correctly. Frames that sit too high or low shift the optical centres away from your pupils, causing blurry vision and eye strain. Frames that are too wide slide down your nose, while tight frames create pressure headaches behind the ears.

Three measurements define frame fit: lens width (the horizontal size of each lens opening), bridge width (the gap between the two lenses), and temple length (the arm that hooks behind your ear). These are printed on the inside of the temple arm as three numbers like 52-18-140. Your optician uses these alongside your face measurements to recommend frames that balance aesthetics and function.

This is why in-person fitting matters, especially for progressive lenses where vertical lens alignment is critical. At Charm Optical in Edmonton, the opticians take precise measurements using digital tools to ensure your frames and lenses work together perfectly. With over 500 frames from brands like Ray-Ban, Oakley, and Versace, you'll find the right combination of style and fit. Most styles are available for same-day glasses at Charm Optical with single vision lenses.

Your eyes are one of the most important tools you have, and they deserve proper care and correction. The technology behind modern eyewear has advanced remarkably in recent years — from digital freeform progressive lenses that customize the optics to your exact wearing position, to lightweight titanium frames that weigh less than a coin, to anti-reflective coatings that virtually eliminate glare. Understanding these options empowers you to make better choices about your eye health and get glasses that genuinely improve your quality of life rather than just meeting a basic need.

Regular eye exams are the foundation of good vision care. The Canadian Association of Optometrists recommends exams every one to two years for adults and annually for children, seniors over 65, and anyone with diabetes or a family history of eye disease. A comprehensive exam does far more than measure your prescription — your optometrist checks the health of your retina, optic nerve, and blood vessels, catching conditions like glaucoma and macular degeneration in their earliest stages. Many serious eye conditions have no noticeable symptoms until significant damage has occurred, making preventive exams essential.

The best next step is always a professional eye exam. Your optometrist can evaluate your specific needs and recommend the right combination of lenses, coatings, and frames for your lifestyle and budget. In Alberta, most employer insurance plans cover comprehensive eye exams, and children under 18 and adults over 65 receive coverage through Alberta Health.

Charm Optical at 5035 Ellerslie Rd SW in South Edmonton offers thorough eye exams with experienced optometrists, a selection of over 500 designer frames from brands like Ray-Ban, Oakley, and Versace, and same-day glasses for many prescription types. They offer direct insurance billing for most Alberta providers, so you only pay the difference at the counter.

Whether you are buying your first pair of glasses, upgrading to progressive lenses, or just need an adjustment on your current frames, having a trusted local optician makes the process smoother. Book an appointment online — most appointments are available within a few days, and walk-ins are welcome for adjustments and frame browsing.

faqs.q: Is Osiris a good glasses brand?
faqs.a: Osiris is a solid mid-range house brand made by Specsavers. The frames are well-made for their price point and offer trendy designs. They're a good choice in the UK where Specsavers operates, but they're not available in Canada.

faqs.q: Where can I buy Osiris glasses in Canada?
faqs.a: Osiris is a Specsavers-exclusive brand, and Specsavers doesn't operate in Canada. Similar styles are available from brands like Calvin Klein, Nine West, and Ray-Ban at Canadian optical stores like Charm Optical in Edmonton.

faqs.q: What brands are similar to Osiris glasses?
faqs.a: Calvin Klein, Nine West, Polo Ralph Lauren, and Ray-Ban offer similar style ranges at comparable or slightly higher price points. Visit Charm Optical in South Edmonton to try on frames from these brands and find your match.

faqs.q: Can I bring frames from the UK and get lenses fitted in Canada?
faqs.a: Most Canadian opticians can fit prescription lenses into frames purchased elsewhere, as long as the frames are in good condition and compatible with the lens type you need. Bring them to your eye exam and your optician can confirm whether they'll work.

faqs.q: How do I know my glasses frame size?
faqs.a: Look inside the temple arm for three numbers (e.g., 52-18-140). The first is lens width in mm, second is bridge width, third is temple length. If you don't have a current pair, an optician at Charm Optical can measure your face to determine the ideal frame dimensions.

faqs.q: What frame material is most durable?
faqs.a: Titanium is the most durable frame material — it's lightweight, corrosion-resistant, hypoallergenic, and flexes without breaking. For budget durability, TR-90 nylon is nearly indestructible. Acetate offers the best colour variety. Charm Optical carries all three materials across their 500+ frame collection.
